reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails significantly on Content Accuracy. The user explicitly commanded the model to read a specific sentence, but Model 1 generated a completely different, embellished script. For example, at 00:03, it inserts '我捧在手里都舍不得放' (I can't bear to put it down), and at 00:10, it adds '阳光一照' (When the sun shines), continuing to 00:15 with '像你一样让我心里都化了'. None of these segments exist in the source text. While the 'sweet' tone quality (音质) appears to be present in the expressive delivery style, the failure to strictly follow the text constraint constitutes a major issue. According to the criteria, strict adherence to the script is the first priority, and this hallucination heavily penalizes the score.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 demonstrates perfect Content Accuracy. It recites the script verbatim from 00:00 to 00:08 ('亲爱的，你送我的这束花好漂亮呀... 整个房间都变得香香甜甜的') without any additions, omissions, or errors. The pacing and flow appear natural. The model successfully integrates the required 'sweet' tone quality (音质) into the correct text, satisfying the core paralinguistic requirement. By meeting both the text constraints and the style instruction simultaneously without flaw, it achieves a fully correct performance.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner due to its strict adherence to the provided text (Content Accuracy). Model 1 failed the fundamental task of reading the prescribed script, instead performing a creative expansion/hallucination of the text. While Model 1 might have sounded sweet, its failure to control the content makes it unsuitable for the task compared to Model 2, which correctly delivered the target content with the target tone.
